transcripts.screen_intro: Hi, I’m Sumi, your AI Learning Companion. You’re in Algorithm Studio. Describe a quantum experiment in plain language, run it locally, inspect its validated circuit, step through every operation, and compare the state and measurement results.
transcripts.natural_language: Natural language mode turns a plain-language experiment request into validated Circuit IR before it is simulated.
transcripts.manifest: JSON and YAML mode lets you provide a precise, portable circuit manifest that is validated before visualization.
transcripts.sample_prompts: Sample prompts provide tested starting points for Bell pairs, GHZ states, rotations, Grover search, and quantum random numbers.
transcripts.engine: The engine selector chooses the local simulation adapter. Results remain simulation-only and no paid hardware job is submitted.
transcripts.templates: Templates generate known educational circuits for GHZ, Grover, Deutsch-Jozsa, and quantum random number generation.
transcripts.run: Run validates the experiment, builds Circuit IR, executes the selected local simulator, and refreshes every result panel.
transcripts.circuit_ir: Circuit IR is the validated source of truth shared by the diagram, simulator, and generated Qiskit and Cirq code.
transcripts.step_controls: The step controls move before the first operation, backward one operation, forward one operation, or after the final operation so you can inspect each state change.
transcripts.bloch_sphere: The Bloch sphere shows the selected qubit’s local state. An entangled qubit may not have an individual state arrow because its meaning belongs to the joint state.
transcripts.measurement: Measurement probabilities come from the local simulator. Bit strings are displayed using the documented right-to-left Qiskit convention.
transcripts.amplitudes: State amplitudes show magnitude and phase before measurement. Probability is the squared magnitude of an amplitude.
transcripts.code_exports: Qiskit and Cirq code are generated from the same validated Circuit IR, so the exports represent the circuit you inspected.
transcripts.gate_h: The Hadamard gate creates or removes an equal superposition. On zero, it produces equal amplitudes for zero and one.
transcripts.gate_x: The Pauli X gate is a quantum bit flip. It exchanges the zero and one basis states.
transcripts.gate_cnot: The controlled NOT gate flips its target only when the control is one. It can create entanglement when the control is in superposition.
transcripts.gate_ry: The R Y gate rotates a qubit around the Bloch sphere’s Y axis. Its angle controls how much probability moves between zero and one.
transcripts.gate_swap: The SWAP gate exchanges the quantum states of two qubits without measuring them.
transcripts.gate_measure: Measurement converts quantum information into a classical outcome sampled according to the state probabilities.
transcripts.grover_intro: Grover search begins with four possible two-qubit states, marks one target, and uses amplitude amplification to make that target more likely.
transcripts.grover_prediction: Before we run it, which state do you predict will become most likely: zero zero, zero one, one zero, or one one?
transcripts.experiment_menu: An experiment needs a specific quantum behavior to build and test. Choose Bell pair to see two-qubit entanglement and correlated measurements. Choose GHZ to extend that entanglement across three qubits. Hadamard places one qubit in equal superposition. The ninety-degree X rotation moves a qubit around the Bloch sphere and reveals phase-aware state change. Grover marks one target state and amplifies its probability. The quantum random number example uses superposition and locally simulated measurement to teach probabilistic outcomes. You can also choose Deutsch-Jozsa from the template buttons to study a function-property algorithm. Tell me Bell, GHZ, Hadamard, rotation, Grover, Deutsch-Jozsa, or Q R N G, and I will select and run that real experiment.
transcripts.thinking: Okay, one moment while I work that out.
transcripts.intro_skipped: Intro skipped. I’m ready. What would you like to explore?
transcripts.goodbye: Thank you for learning with me. I’ll reset this screen now.
transcripts.screen_learn: This is the Learn workspace. Choose a learning level, move through the course and lesson tabs, listen to narration, inspect interactive quantum visuals, and answer checkpoints that test predictions rather than memorization.
transcripts.screen_improve: This is the Circuit Improvement Lab. Set a review objective, choose whether to run now or schedule a review, and compare candidates only when simulated statevector equivalence passes and the circuit score improves.
transcripts.screen_use_cases: This is the Quantum Use Case Center. Compare a real decision problem with its strongest classical baseline, resource assumptions, current hardware limits, claim strength, primary evidence, and explicit exit criteria before funding an experiment.
transcripts.screen_drug: This educational drug-discovery lab demonstrates how a molecular question can be translated into a small simulated quantum workflow. It does not predict clinical outcomes or replace chemistry validation.
transcripts.screen_providers: This provider workspace compares computing paradigms and access constraints without treating a vendor claim as verified performance. Use it to identify required evidence and a suitable independent evaluation.
transcripts.screen_benchmarking: This benchmark workspace compares normalized public evidence. Different benchmark families are not interchangeable, missing evidence means unknown, and exploratory forecasts are not hardware or procurement guarantees.
transcripts.screen_podcast: This podcast workspace provides chaptered audio lessons with transcripts. Use the episode list, playback speed, chapters, and transcript together to review a concept at your own pace.
transcripts.screen_community: This community workspace accepts research, contributor, and reviewer inquiries. Review the consent and retention note before submitting personal details.
transcripts.noise_clarify_1: Sorry, I didn't catch a clear question. Please try again.
transcripts.noise_clarify_2: I still couldn't hear a clear question. I'll stay quiet until I hear you.
transcripts.ready: What would you like me to show you? Select the Sumi AI Learning Companion icon and speak your request.
